avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow will fall until midday in particular along the border with France, along the border between Valais and Italy.

The strong wind will transport the new snow. As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls and in areas close to the tree line, and in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

On wind-loaded slopes and on very steep slopes medium-sized to large slab avalanches are possible, in isolated cases also in case of releases originating from steep and high-altitude southeast facing starting zones that have retained the snow thus far.
avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 25 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday. Some snow will fall until midday.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. They are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1000 m. Little snow will fall in the late morning in some localities.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a gathering strong wind from westerly directions, clearly visible wind slabs will form at elevated altitudes.
Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant, especially on steep shady slopes in high Alpine regions, as well as on wind-loaded slopes. Caution is to be exercised at transitions into gullies and bowls.

In isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

As a consequence of falling temperatures, the activity of moist and wet avalanches will decrease.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs in the high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the loosely bonded old snow. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
As a consequence of a moderate wind from northwesterly directions, mostly small wind slabs will form.

Low and intermediate altitudes: As a consequence of falling temperatures and the strong westerly wind, a crust will form on the surface during the night.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind will transport the fresh and old snow. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form in the course of the day. These will form in particular between approximately 2100 and 2500 m.
The wind slabs are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. They can be released even by a single winter sport participant.
Caution is to be exercised in particular on slopes, and on wind-loaded slopes in particular above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind will be strong to storm force at times. Wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 25 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1400 m. The strong wind will transport the new snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.
